industry_trends: Shift from self-serve to consultative sales in martech
Increasing average deal sizes in email marketing platforms
Privacy regulation (GDPR, state-level laws) driving first-party data strategies

competitor_moves.name: MailReach
competitor_moves.action: Launched enterprise tier with dedicated account managers

competitor_moves.name: SendGrid Pro
competitor_moves.action: Extended free trial from 14 to 30 days

competitor_moves.name: TargetImpact
competitor_moves.action: Expanded sales team by 20% in Q1

competitor_moves.name: CampaignForge
competitor_moves.action: Acquired data enrichment startup DataPulse for $18M

competitor_moves.name: Drip+
competitor_moves.action: Announced partnership with Shopify for native integration

analyst_outlook: The email marketing SaaS space is experiencing a notable shift toward higher-touch, consultative sales models. Vendors that previously relied on self-serve funnels are investing in sales teams and extending consultation offerings to capture larger deals. This trend is expected to continue through 2024 as average contract values rise and buyer expectations increase.
